## Frequently Asked Questions

My colleagues and I already have Otter accounts. Can we switch to Business?

Yes! Just log in to your account, go to **Settings**, and select **“Upgrade to Business”**. Follow the prompts and you’ll be set.

What payment methods do you accept?

We accept credit and debit cards on the [Otter.ai](/) website, as well as in-app purchases billed through the Apple App Store or Google Play.

Can I pay with PayPal?

Not yet — but we’re actively considering adding PayPal as a payment option in the future.

Do you offer student discounts?

Yes, we offer a 20% discount on Otter Pro Monthly and Otter Pro Annual plans for **individual** students and teachers using an email address ending with **.edu**. The discounted prices are as follows:

Otter Pro Annual: $6.67 USD per month (billed $79.99 annually)
Otter Pro Monthly: $13.59 USD per month

**To subscribe:**
1. [Sign up](/signup) for Otter Basic (or [login](/signin)) using an email address ending with .edu (e.g., john.smith@stanford.edu).
2. After you’ve signed into your Otter account, click your name in the top left, and then click the “[Upgrade plan](/setting/plan)” link.
3. Click the “Student & Teacher Discount” link towards the bottom of the page, just below the Pro plan description.

For additional details, please see our [help center article](https://help.otter.ai/hc/en-us/articles/4402467517847-Purchase-the-Pro-plan-with-the-Student-Teacher-discount).

## **How Much Does Otter.ai Cost?**

[Otter.ai](http://otter.ai/) costs $0 for the Basic plan, $8.33 to $16.99/month for Pro, $19.99 to $30/user/month for Business, and custom pricing for Enterprise, with third-party data reporting average enterprise contracts in the mid-four figures annually. Annual billing on Pro saves about 51% versus monthly.

That’s the headline, but the per-plan reality matters more than the number on the pricing page. Each tier has different minute caps, conversation length limits, and file import allowances, and those limits are where most buyers get surprised.

### **Basic (Free) Plan — 300 Minutes/Month**

The free plan gives you 300 transcription minutes per month, capped at 30 minutes per conversation. You also get only three lifetime file imports, not three per month, three ever. For most knowledge workers who upload pre-recorded audio or video, the free tier evaporates within a week of trying it.

It’s a true tire-kicker tier: enough to test the speaker diarization quality and the Zoom/Meet/Teams integration, but not enough for any consistent workflow.

### **Pro Plan — $16.99/mo Monthly or $8.33/mo Annual**

Otter Pro is the most popular paid tier. As of 2026, Pro includes 1,200 monthly transcription minutes (about 20 hours), 90 minutes per conversation, and 10 file imports per month. Annual billing brings the effective cost to $99.99/year, a roughly 51% discount versus paying monthly.

### **Business Plan — $30/user/mo Monthly or $19.99/user/mo Annual**

Business is where Otter starts to make sense for sales and customer success teams running daily Zoom calls. You get unlimited meetings and in-app recording transcription, up to 6,000 imported-file minutes per user per month, a 4-hour per-conversation cap, advanced search across transcripts, admin controls, and shared custom vocabulary. Annual billing puts the per-seat cost at $19.99/user/month, or about $240/user/year.

For a 5-seat sales team on annual billing, Business runs roughly $1,200/year, which is reasonable if your team actually fills those minutes. The break-even versus Pro is one user generating more than 1,200 minutes per month or needing conversations longer than 90 minutes.

### **Enterprise Plan — Custom Pricing**

Enterprise is custom-priced and built for legal, healthcare, and large sales orgs that need SSO, advanced security controls, dedicated support, and bulk seat licensing. Third-party datasets (including SpendHound and CostBench) report enterprise contracts often landing in the mid-four figures annually, with larger contracts going higher. There’s no public price card for Enterprise; all pricing runs through Otter’s sales team.

## **The Hidden Costs of Otter.ai: Most Reviews Skip**

The list price is the easy part. The real cost shows up in the limits buyers don’t notice until they hit them.

### **File Import Caps and Forced Business Upgrades**

Pro caps file imports at 10 per month. For a journalist, podcaster, or researcher who uploads pre-recorded audio rather than capturing live meetings, that ceiling is limiting fast. Hit it and your only option is jumping to Business at roughly 2x the per-seat cost. There is no a la carte file import add-on.

### **Language Support Limits Global ROI**

Otter supports transcription in English, French, and Spanish. For a U.S.-focused team working only in those languages, that’s workable. For any team with Portuguese, German, Mandarin, Japanese, Arabic, or other meetings, you’re paying full price for partial coverage and routing those calls to a second tool. This is a key reason multilingual teams move to platforms like [Sonix](https://sonix.ai/), which supports [53+ languages](https://sonix.ai/languages) and bundles [automated translation](https://sonix.ai/translation) in the same paid hour.

### **Subscription Caps vs. Usage-Based Reality**

Otter’s pricing model is subscription-capped: you pay for a bucket of minutes, and if you hit your limits, you may need to wait for reset or upgrade your plan. Usage-based competitors charge per audio hour, so a slow month costs less and a busy month doesn’t trigger a forced tier change. The market is moving in this direction. Fathom’s Free plan includes unlimited recordings and transcription (per Fathom’s pricing page), and Fireflies offers unlimited transcription on its Pro tier. Otter’s minute caps are starting to look out of step with where the category is heading.

For teams with variable workloads, usage-based pricing is often structurally cheaper because you’re billed for the audio you actually transcribe, not a prepaid allotment.

WHAT OTTER ACTUALLY COSTS FOR TEAMS

| Team size | Pro (monthly) | Pro (annual) | Business (monthly) | Business (annual) |

| 1 person | $16.99 | $8.33 | $30 | $19.99 |

| 10 people | $169.90 | $83.30 | $300 | $199.90 |
| 25 people | $424.75 | $208.25 | $750 | $499.75 |

HIDDEN COSTS & LIMITATIONS

Pro plan minutes reduced by 80%

Otter reduced the Pro plan from 6,000 to 1,200 minutes per month without a price cut. If you have 5+ meetings per day, you'll burn through 1,200 minutes in about two weeks.

Only 10 file imports per month on Pro

Upload recordings from Zoom, Loom, or other tools? You're capped at 10 per month on Pro. Need more? Upgrade to Business.

Sales Notetaker locked behind Enterprise

Otter's AI-powered sales features (OtterPilot for Sales) require the Enterprise plan with custom pricing. Not available on Pro or Business.

No refunds for mid-cycle cancellations

Cancel an annual plan mid-year and you don't get a prorated refund. Access continues until the billing period ends, but the money is gone.

Visible bot joins every meeting

"Otter.ai" appears as a participant in your call. Clients and prospects notice. Not a pricing issue, but a cost in meeting dynamics.

# Otter.ai Pricing 2026: Is the Pro Plan Worth It?

[Ai Transcription](/blog/tags/ai-transcription)[Transcription Accuracy](/blog/tags/transcription-accuracy)[Meeting Transcription](/blog/tags/meeting-transcription)

Otter.ai pricing shows $8.33-16.99 per user/month for the Pro plan with 1,200 monthly minutes, or $20-30 per user/month for Business with 6,000 minutes. Those rates sound reasonable—until you realize that **Otter.ai is subscription-only with hard minute caps**, not true usage-based pricing.

Need to transcribe 1,500 minutes one month but only 400 the next? Too bad. You're paying the same subscription fee regardless of actual usage. Hit your minute cap mid-month? Your options are upgrade to a more expensive tier or wait until next month. This isn't usage-based pricing—it's **subscription pricing with usage limits**.

True usage-based pricing means paying only for what you use: transcribe 200 minutes, pay for 200 minutes. Transcribe 2,000 minutes, pay for 2,000. No monthly caps, no wasted subscription fees during slow months, no surprise tier upgrades when you exceed limits.

For [comparing transcription pricing](/blog/ai-transcription-pricing-deals-2025-cost-comparison) across all major services, see our comprehensive cost analysis.

In this guide, we'll break down Otter.ai's complete 2025 pricing across all subscription tiers, calculate the effective per-minute costs with minute caps included, reveal the hidden limitations of subscription models, and show you a true usage-based $6.00 flat rate alternative with no monthly caps or subscription fees.

## Quick Navigation

* [Otter.ai Pricing Overview (2025)](#otterai-pricing-overview-2025)
* [The Subscription vs Usage-Based Trap](#the-subscription-vs-usage-based-trap)
* [Hidden Costs & Limitations Most Users Miss](#hidden-costs-limitations-most-users-miss)
* [Subscription vs Usage-Based: When Each Makes Sense](#subscription-vs-usage-based-when-each-makes-sense)
* [Otter.ai vs BrassTranscripts: Cost Scenarios](#otterai-vs-brasstranscripts-cost-scenarios)
* [When to Choose Otter.ai vs BrassTranscripts](#when-to-choose-otterai-vs-brasstranscripts)
* [Frequently Asked Questions](#frequently-asked-questions)
* [AI Prompt: Otter.ai Pricing Calculator](#ai-prompt-otterai-pricing-calculator)
* [Final Verdict: Otter.ai vs True Usage-Based Pricing](#final-verdict-otterai-vs-true-usage-based-pricing)
* [Pricing Disclaimer](#pricing-disclaimer)

## Otter.ai Pricing Overview (2025)

According to Otter.ai's official pricing page (verified October 2025):

### Subscription Plans

| Plan | Monthly Price | Annual Price (per month) | Minutes Included | Per-Meeting Limit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Basic (Free)** | $0 | $0 | 300/month | 30 min/meeting |
| **Pro** | $16.99/user | **$8.33/user** | 1,200/month | 90 min/meeting |
| **Business** | $30/user | **$20/user** | 6,000/month | 4 hours/meeting |
| **Enterprise** | Custom | Custom | Custom | Custom |

*Last verified: October 24, 2025 from Otter.ai official pricing page*

**Critical limitation:** These are **subscription plans with minute caps**, not usage-based pricing. You pay the monthly/annual fee regardless of actual usage.

### Effective Per-Minute Costs

Let's calculate what you're actually paying per minute if you use your full allocation:

**Pro Plan (Annual - $8.33/month):**

```
$99.96/year ÷ 14,400 minutes/year = $0.007/min (if you use all 1,200 min/month)
```

**Business Plan (Annual - $20/month):**

```
$240/year ÷ 72,000 minutes/year = $0.003/min (if you use all 6,000 min/month)
```

But here's the problem: **If you only use 50% of your allocation, your effective rate doubles.**

**Pro Plan using only 600 min/month:**

```
$99.96/year ÷ 7,200 minutes/year = $0.014/min (2x more expensive)
```

### File Import Limitations

| Plan | Audio/Video File Imports |
| --- | --- |
| Basic | 3 files (lifetime total) |
| Pro | 10 files/month |
| Business | Unlimited files |

**Gotcha:** Even Pro users are limited to 10 file imports per month. For podcast producers or content creators with 20+ episodes/month, this forces an upgrade to Business ($20/user/month minimum).

## The Subscription vs Usage-Based Trap

Otter.ai's pricing model is **subscription-based masquerading as usage-based**. Here's why that matters:

### True Usage-Based Pricing

**How it works:**

* Transcribe 100 minutes → Pay for 100 minutes
* Transcribe 2,000 minutes → Pay for 2,000 minutes
* No monthly minimums, no caps, no wasted fees

**Examples:**

* AWS Transcribe: $0.024/min (pure usage-based)
* Deepgram: $0.0043/min (pure usage-based)
* AssemblyAI: $0.0025/min (pure usage-based)
* BrassTranscripts: $2.50 for 0-15 min, $6.00 flat rate for 16+ min (pure usage-based, no account needed)

### Subscription-Based Pricing (Otter.ai)

**How it works:**

* Pay $8.33/month → Get up to 1,200 minutes
* Use 200 minutes → Still pay $8.33 (effective rate: $0.042/min)
* Use 1,200 minutes → Pay $8.33 (effective rate: $0.007/min)
* Need 1,300 minutes → Must upgrade to Business ($20/month)

**The trap:** Your effective per-minute cost fluctuates wildly based on utilization rate.

### Real-World Cost Comparison: Variable Usage

**Scenario:** Podcast producer with variable monthly volume:

* January: 800 minutes (4 episodes × 200 min each)
* February: 400 minutes (2 episodes × 200 min each)
* March: 1,500 minutes (6 episodes × 250 min each)
* April: 600 minutes (3 episodes × 200 min each)

**Otter.ai Pro Plan ($8.33/month annual):**

```
January: $8.33 (800 min used = $0.010/min effective)
February: $8.33 (400 min used = $0.021/min effective)
March: Must upgrade to Business $20 (exceeded 1,200 min cap)
         OR wait until next month (unacceptable for production)
April: $8.33 (600 min used = $0.014/min effective)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Q1 Total: $36.86 (2,700 min if March requires upgrade to Business)
Effective rate: $0.014-0.021/min (highly variable)
March problem: Either pay $20 (2.4x monthly rate) or delay production
```

**True Usage-Based Alternative (BrassTranscripts $2.50 for 0-15 min, $6.00 flat rate for 16+ min):**

```
January: 4 files × $6.00 = $20
February: 2 files × $6.00 = $10
March: 6 files × $6.00 = $30
April: 3 files × $6.00 = $15
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Q1 Total: $75
Flat rate: $6.00 per file (constant, predictable)
No caps, no tier changes, no delayed production
```

**Analysis:** Otter.ai is cheaper ($36.86 vs $75)—but only if you accept:

* Forced subscription commitment
* Minute caps requiring tier upgrades during high-volume months
* Wasted subscription fees during low-usage months
* 10 file import limit (Pro) forcing Business upgrade

## Hidden Costs & Limitations Most Users Miss

### 1. Per-User Pricing Multiplier

Otter.ai charges **per user, not per workspace**. A 5-person team using Otter.ai Business:

```
5 users × $20/month (annual) = $100/month
5 users × 6,000 minutes = 30,000 minutes/month capacity
```

**But:** Most teams don't need each user to have 6,000 personal minutes. They need a **shared pool** of transcription capacity.

**Impact:** For teams, per-user pricing forces you to pay for unused capacity across multiple users.

### 2. The 30-Minute Meeting Trap (Free Plan)

Otter.ai's Free plan caps **individual meetings at 30 minutes**. This means:

* 90-minute team meeting → Requires manual restarts every 30 minutes (3 separate transcripts)
* Cannot transcribe a single 60-minute podcast episode on Free plan

**Workaround:** Split long recordings into 30-minute chunks (annoying and error-prone).

### 3. File Import Limits Force Tier Upgrades

* **Basic:** 3 file imports (lifetime total)
* **Pro:** 10 file imports/month
* **Business:** Unlimited file imports

For content creators producing 20+ podcast episodes, YouTube videos, or interviews monthly, **Pro's 10-file limit forces a Business upgrade** ($20/month vs $8.33/month = 2.4x cost increase).

### 4. Mobile App Upload Limits

Otter.ai's mobile apps have additional limitations:

* Free plan: 30-minute conversation limit
* File size limits on mobile uploads

**Impact:** Users often hit limits not mentioned on pricing page.

### 5. Overage Charges? No—Hard Caps

Unlike some subscription services, Otter.ai does NOT offer overage pricing. When you hit your minute cap:

* ❌ Cannot transcribe additional audio until next month
* ❌ No "pay extra for additional minutes" option
* ✅ Only option: Upgrade to higher tier mid-month (prorated billing)

**Example:** Pro user hits 1,200-minute cap on day 20 of the month. Options:

1. Wait 10 days until next month (unacceptable for production workflows)
2. Upgrade to Business ($20/month prorated for remaining 10 days)

This creates unpredictable monthly costs for variable-volume users.

### 6. Annual Billing Lock-In

Otter.ai's best rates require **annual commitment**:

* Pro: $8.33/month (annual) vs $16.99/month (monthly) = 2x more expensive monthly
* Business: $20/month (annual) vs $30/month (monthly) = 1.5x more expensive monthly

**Risk:** Commit to annual plan, realize it doesn't fit your workflow → stuck paying for 12 months or lose prepayment.

## Subscription vs Usage-Based: When Each Makes Sense

### When Otter.ai's Subscription Model Wins

**✅ Predictable, high-volume usage close to tier limits**

If you consistently use 1,000-1,200 minutes/month (Pro) or 5,000-6,000 minutes/month (Business), subscription pricing delivers the lowest effective per-minute rate.

**Example:** Corporate team with daily standups, weekly all-hands, monthly reviews = 1,100 minutes/month consistently.

* Otter.ai Pro (annual): $8.33/month = $0.0076/min effective
* True usage-based at $6.00 flat rate: Depends on file count

**Winner:** Otter.ai for predictable high-volume usage with many files.

**✅ Team collaboration features prioritized**

Otter.ai includes:

* Real-time collaboration (multiple users editing live transcript)
* Meeting assistant integration (Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams)
* OtterPilot automated meeting joining
* Shared workspaces

If these collaboration features are critical, Otter.ai's subscription model provides them bundled with transcription.

**✅ Small teams under 5 users**

For 1-5 users with predictable usage, Otter.ai's per-user pricing is manageable:

* 3 users × $8.33 (Pro annual) = $25/month for 3,600 minutes total capacity

### When True Usage-Based Pricing Wins

**✅ Variable monthly volume**

If your transcription needs fluctuate (100 minutes some months, 2,000 others), subscription models waste money during slow months and force tier upgrades during high-volume months.

**Example:** Podcast producer alternating between 400 min/month (2 episodes) and 1,400 min/month (7 episodes).

Otter.ai Pro: Wasted capacity in slow months, forced Business upgrade in high-volume months.

True usage-based: Pay exactly for what you use, no wasted fees, no caps.

**✅ Unpredictable usage patterns**

Agencies, freelancers, or businesses with project-based transcription:

* Some months: No transcription needed
* Other months: Surge usage for client projects

**Subscription waste:** Paying $8.33-20/month even when you use 0 minutes.

**✅ Large file imports (10+ files/month)**

Otter.ai Pro limits file imports to 10/month. For content creators with 20+ episodes, interviews, or videos monthly, this forces a Business upgrade ($20/month).

True usage-based services have no file import caps—just pay per file transcribed.

**✅ No team collaboration features needed**

If you just need transcripts (not real-time collaboration, OtterPilot, or workspace features), subscription overhead provides features you don't use.

**✅ No account/subscription management wanted**

BrassTranscripts: Upload file → Download transcript. No account creation, no subscription management, no billing cycle.

Otter.ai: Account creation, subscription selection, payment method, monthly/annual decision, tier monitoring.

## Otter.ai vs BrassTranscripts: Cost Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Solo Podcaster (Variable Volume)

**Requirements:**

* 3 podcast episodes/month average
* But varies: 2-6 episodes depending on production schedule
* Need speaker identification
* Non-technical user

**Otter.ai Pro (Annual - $8.33/month):**

```
Subscription: $8.33/month fixed
Variable file count: 2-6 episodes/month
Covers up to 1,200 min/month ✅
File imports: Max 10/month ✅
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Annual cost: $99.96
Per-file cost: Varies based on utilization
```

**BrassTranscripts ($2.50 for 0-15 min, $6.00 flat rate for 16+ min):**

```
2-episode months: 2 × $6.00 = $10
6-episode months: 6 × $6.00 = $30
Average (3-4 episodes): $15-20/month
Speaker ID: Included
No file import limits
No account needed: Upload and go
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Annual cost (3 episodes/month avg): $180
Flat rate: $6.00/file constant
```

**Winner:** Otter.ai is cheaper ($99.96 vs $180 annually) IF you accept:

* Annual subscription commitment
* Minute caps requiring careful monitoring
* 10 file import limit
* Must stay within 1,200 min/month or upgrade

**BrassTranscripts wins if:**

* You value zero subscription management
* You need no file import caps
* You prefer no account needed workflow
* You want no minute monitoring

### Scenario 2: Corporate Team (5 Users, Predictable Usage)

**Requirements:**

* 5 users needing transcription
* 800 minutes/month per user average (4,000 total)
* Daily meetings, weekly reviews

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How accurate is Otter.ai compared to competitors?

Otter.ai achieves good accuracy on clear English audio, comparable to most AI transcription services. Accuracy depends on audio quality, accents, background noise, and technical terminology. For specialized content (medical, legal), competitors with custom models may outperform Otter.ai.

### Does Otter.ai include speaker identification?

Yes. Otter.ai includes speaker diarization (identifying different speakers) in all paid plans. Free plan also supports speaker ID but with 30-minute meeting limits.

### What happens when I hit my minute cap mid-month?

You cannot transcribe additional audio until next month, OR you must upgrade to a higher tier (prorated billing for remainder of month). Otter.ai does NOT offer overage charges—it's hard caps only.

### Can I downgrade my plan if I'm not using all my minutes?

Yes, but changes typically take effect at the next billing cycle. If you're on annual billing, you may need to wait until renewal to downgrade.

### Does Otter.ai support languages other than English?

Otter.ai primarily supports English transcription. Limited support for Spanish and some other languages exists, but English delivers the best accuracy. For multi-language transcription, consider Whisper API (50+ languages) or Deepgram.

### How does Otter.ai pricing compare to competitors?

**Effective per-minute costs (if you use full allocation):**

* Otter.ai Pro (annual): $0.007/min (1,200 min/month)
* Otter.ai Business (annual): $0.003/min (6,000 min/month)
* Deepgram: $0.0043/min (pure usage-based)
* AssemblyAI: $0.0025/min (pure usage-based)
* BrassTranscripts: $2.50 for 0-15 min, $6.00 flat rate for 16+ min (pure usage-based with speaker ID)

Otter.ai is cheapest **IF** you consistently use near your tier limits. Competitors are cheaper for variable or low-volume usage.

### What's included in Otter.ai's free plan?

* 300 minutes/month
* 30 minutes per conversation (hard limit)
* 3 audio/video file imports (lifetime total)
* Basic transcription with speaker ID
* 600 minutes of AI summaries/month

Free plan is viable for light usage but 30-minute conversation limit is restrictive.

### Can teams share a pool of minutes or is it per-user?

**Per-user allocation.** Each user on a paid plan gets their own minute allocation:

* Pro: 1,200 min/user/month (not pooled)
* Business: 6,000 min/user/month (not pooled)

**Impact:** 5-user team cannot pool 30,000 minutes (5 × 6,000). Each user has individual 6,000-minute cap.

### Does Otter.ai work with Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams?

Yes. Otter.ai integrates with:

* Zoom (OtterPilot auto-joins meetings)
* Google Meet
* Microsoft Teams

These integrations auto-record and transcribe meetings without manual uploads. Feature availability varies by plan tier.

### How does annual vs monthly billing affect pricing?

**Pro Plan:**

* Annual: $8.33/month ($99.96/year)
* Monthly: $16.99/month
* **Savings:** 51% cheaper with annual commitment

**Business Plan:**

* Annual: $20/month ($240/year)
* Monthly: $30/month
* **Savings:** 33% cheaper with annual commitment

Annual billing locks you in for 12 months but delivers significant savings.

### Can I export transcripts in different formats?

Yes. Otter.ai supports export to:

* Text (.txt)
* PDF
* SRT (subtitles)
* DOCX (Word)
* JSON (via API on Business/Enterprise)

Export capabilities vary by plan tier—check documentation for current limits.

### Does Otter.ai offer refunds if I'm not satisfied?

Otter.ai's refund policy varies. Generally, monthly subscriptions can be canceled anytime, but annual prepayments may have limited refund windows. Check their terms of service for current refund policy.

## Final Verdict: Otter.ai vs True Usage-Based Pricing

Otter.ai delivers exceptional value for **teams with predictable, high-volume transcription needs** who value real-time collaboration features. If you consistently use 1,000-1,200 minutes/month (Pro) or 5,000-6,000 minutes/month (Business), Otter.ai's effective per-minute rate ($0.003-0.007/min) beats nearly all competitors.

**Choose Otter.ai if:**

* You have predictable, high-volume usage near tier limits
* You need real-time collaboration (live editing, OtterPilot, workspace features)
* You're a corporate team with daily meetings

# Otter.ai Pricing: Free Limits, Pro & Business Plans in 2026 Is It Worth It in 2026?

Otter free: 300 min/mo with a 30-min cap. Pro: $16.99/mo ($8.33 annual). Business: $30/user. Every plan's limits, caps, and what you actually get.

2026-02-24     Updated 2026-03-17     5 min read

Quick Answer

How much does Otter.ai cost in 2026?

* Free (Basic): 300 minutes/month total, 30-minute cap per conversation, suitable only for light use
* Pro: $16.99/month (or $8.33/month billed annually), 1,200 minutes/month, 90-minute conversations
* Business: $30/user/month, adds admin controls, custom vocabulary, and advanced integrations
* Enterprise: custom pricing for large organizations with SSO, compliance, and priority support

Otter.ai's per-minute model makes pricing predictable but also means heavy users will hit monthly caps on every tier. The annual Pro plan at $8.33/month is exceptional value if your usage stays under 1,200 minutes per month.

TL;DR

Otter.ai costs $16.99/month for Pro ($8.33/month billed annually) with 1,200 transcription minutes and 90-minute conversation caps. The free plan gives you 300 minutes/month with a 30-minute cap per conversation. Business is $30/user/month for teams needing admin controls and custom vocabulary.

## Otter.ai Pricing Plans at a Glance

Otter.ai structures pricing around transcription minutes: how many minutes you can transcribe per month, and how long any individual conversation can run. This makes it easy to estimate costs but also means every tier has hard ceilings that professional users will encounter.

## Free Plan: Very Limited in Practice

Otter’s free Basic plan is best understood as a trial rather than a working tool for active professionals. The 300-minute monthly cap means you can transcribe roughly 5 hours of audio per month, fine for occasional use, but limiting for anyone in a role with regular meetings.

The 30-minute conversation cap is the more frustrating limit. Most professional meetings run 45–60 minutes. Otter on the free plan will stop transcribing at the 30-minute mark. You’ll either need to manually restart the recording (losing continuity in the transcript) or accept that the second half of every meeting goes unrecorded.

AI Chat is limited to [20 queries per month (max 3 per conversation)](https://otter.ai/pricing) on the free plan, which makes the free tier inadequate for any professional who relies on AI-assisted review rather than reading raw transcripts.

The free plan is good for evaluating Otter’s transcription accuracy and interface. It is not a viable long-term tool for professionals with more than a few meetings per week.

**Comparing costs? alfred\_ is $24.99/mo with no hidden fees.**  [Try now →](https://get-alfred.ai/signup?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=cta-subtle&utm_campaign=otter-pricing&ref=blog)

## Pro Plan ($16.99/month)

Pro raises the monthly cap to 1,200 minutes (20 hours) and extends individual conversation length to 90 minutes. This covers most professional workflows: someone who has 4–5 meetings per week will use roughly 10–15 hours of transcription per month, comfortably within the Pro limit.

The annual billing rate of $8.33/month makes Pro an excellent value proposition. At that price, Otter is one of the most affordable transcription tools in the market, and the transcription accuracy is genuinely strong, particularly for structured meetings with clear speakers.

Where Pro falls short: it’s still a transcription tool, not an action-oriented one. Otter will give you an accurate, searchable record of what was said. It will not intelligently identify which action items belong to you, draft follow-up emails based on the discussion, or push tasks to your project management system.

For researchers, journalists, and anyone who needs high-volume transcription for content work, Otter Pro at $8.33/month annually is hard to beat. For professionals who need the transcript to become actionable work, it’s a starting point rather than a complete solution.

## Business Plan ($30/user/month)

Business raises the monthly cap to 6,000 minutes per user and extends conversation length to 4 hours. It adds admin controls, shared workspace management, custom vocabulary for industry-specific terminology, and integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, and Zapier.

At $30/user/month, Business is priced for organizations where transcription volume is high and centralized management is required. For most small teams, two or three Pro licenses will cover the need at lower cost per seat.

## Hidden Costs

* **1,200-minute cap catches high-volume users.** 1,200 minutes sounds like a lot until you realize that a professional with 5 one-hour meetings per week hits 1,200 minutes in exactly 4 weeks. Heavy meeting periods, conferences, client sprints, quarterly reviews, can exhaust the Pro cap mid-month.
* **Audio import is gated on paid plans.** On the free plan, you can only import 3 audio files for transcription over your entire account lifetime. If you want to use Otter for transcribing recorded interviews, podcast episodes, or voice memos, you must be on Pro.
* **The transcript doesn’t become action.** Otter’s core product is raw transcription. Action items appear in the summary but are not intelligently extracted, prioritized, or pushed to external tools automatically. Every action item still requires a human to read the summary and decide what to do.
* **Integrations limited on lower tiers.** CRM and project management integrations (Salesforce, HubSpot, Zapier workflows) require Business tier. Pro users who want to push meeting data to external tools will find their options limited.

## Is Otter.ai Worth the Price?

At $8.33/month annually, Otter Pro is excellent value for its primary use case: high-accuracy, high-volume transcription. For journalists who conduct hours of interviews, researchers who record focus groups, or content creators who want searchable transcripts of their audio, the price-to-quality ratio is strong.

For corporate professionals who attend structured business meetings, Otter is less compelling relative to competitors like [Fathom](/blog/fathom-pricing) (free for unlimited recording/transcription with basic summaries, but advanced AI summary templates limited to 5 calls/month; individual Premium from $16/month annual) or [tl;dv](https://tldv.io/app/pricing/) (better CRM integration at $18/user/month annually).

Otter’s main weakness in a business context is that it treats transcription as the end product, not the beginning of a workflow. The transcript is accurate. Turning it into action, [emails](/blog/best-ai-email-assistants), tasks, calendar entries, is still entirely manual work.

## The Better-Value Alternative: alfred\_

alfred\_ at $24.99/month approaches the post-meeting workflow from the opposite direction. Rather than producing a transcript and leaving action extraction to you, alfred\_ handles the execution layer: drafting the [follow-up email](/blog/best-ai-meeting-follow-up-tools) to the client, extracting tasks and deadlines from meeting context, managing your calendar for the next call, and [triaging the inbox](/blog/how-to-automate-email-triage) that accumulated while you were on the meeting.

For professionals who already have a transcription tool they’re happy with, alfred\_ isn’t a replacement. It’s the layer that turns captures into completed work. For professionals frustrated that a perfect Otter transcript still requires 45 minutes of follow-up every time, alfred\_ addresses exactly that bottleneck.

alfred\_ offers a 7-day free trial so you can evaluate whether it meaningfully reduces your post-meeting workload before committing.

Our Verdict

Otter Pro at $8.33/month annually is excellent for transcription volume. Not for smart action extraction.

Otter.ai is one of the best-priced transcription services available, particularly on the annual Pro plan at $8.33/month. For business professionals who need meetings to generate action, follow-up emails, tasks, scheduling, Otter's raw transcription model leaves that work entirely manual.
